mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when unemployment stays above 4.0 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail auto sales combined with low federal funds rates indicate robust consumer credit expansion and demand, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS rises but FEDFUNDS also rises, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation (Rank IC < 0.01) with INDPRO momentum over a 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals a strengthening labor market and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or the relationship breaks down when payroll gains exceed 300k without corresponding wage growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 250.0, persistent inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally during high inflation periods, or the threshold signal produces a negative Rank IC (< -0.02) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing starts and industrial production indicates synchronized growth across construction and manufacturing sectors, generating broad-based corporate earnings momentum and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when housing starts rise but industrial production stagnates, or the combined signal fails to beat a baseline model in out-of-sample directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter acceleration in real GDP growth reflects expanding aggregate demand and business investment, which translates into higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to predict positive returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 55% in directional classification over a 5-year rolling window.
